source_note: Actor kinematics exist; counterpart rule and threshold freeze remain required.
Acceleration and speed are directly recorded for ego/world actors.
Derivative extraction requires frozen smoothing and missing-data rules.
Kinematics alone cannot establish forcedness or right-of-way.
Role reversal requires semantic priority and role interpretation.
Speed and timestamp channels support stop-go cycle derivation.
Necessity interpretation lacks route and context signals.
Escalation semantics and thresholds are not automatically supported.
Geometry and actor IDs support near-miss proxy extraction after geometry freeze.
No explicit intervention flag or authoritative status-code dictionary.
No fallback key or documented planner-status semantics.
No plan-update counter or nonempty plan list in inspected logs.
No rejection or validator channel represented in available schema.
No explicit mission failure dictionary or route/goal geometry.
Retained only as geometric contact candidate, not sensor-confirmed collision.
No-progress is retained; off-route remains unavailable without route/lane/goal geometry.
No takeover flag or authoritative status-code dictionary.
Retained only as kinematic emergency-stop candidate.
Lateral acceleration, steering, speed, and timestamp channels exist.
Non-counterpart yielding semantics lack frozen role/context rules.
